rationale: The source describes individual equity technical analysis for swing trading. The PLTR section contains a reasonably mechanical inverted head-and-shoulders breakout setup with stated trigger, target, and a directional invalidation scenario. AMD 464 support and TSLA confirmation-at-support are mentioned but lack complete mechanical rules.

claims.summary: PLTR consolidating between 136 resistance and a rising 21-day EMA, forming an inverted head-and-shoulders pattern with 136 as the neckline.
missing_fields: No volume condition for pattern confirmation

claims.summary: A clear upward breakout above 136 resistance (the inverted H&S neckline) triggers a long entry targeting the 160s. A downward breakout of the 21-day EMA invalidates the bullish thesis and targets a pullback to 105.
claims.trigger_rule: Price makes a clear upward breakout above 136 resistance (inverted H&S neckline level).
claims.invalidation_rule: Price breaks down below the rising 21-day EMA, forming a possible double top pattern and targeting a pullback toward 105.
missing_fields: What constitutes 'clear breakout' is not defined (close above, % threshold, volume filter)
No explicit stop-loss level for the entry
No time limit on holding period

candidate.title: PLTR Resistance Neckline Breakout
candidate.summary: When PLTR is consolidating between a known resistance level (136) and a rising 21-day EMA, with that resistance serving as the neckline of an inverted head-and-shoulders pattern, a clear upward breakout above that level triggers a long entry targeting the prior high zone (~160s). The setup is invalidated if price instead breaks below the 21-day EMA, which targets a deeper pullback toward 105.
candidate.data_requirements: Daily OHLCV for PLTR. No exotic data — standard price, simple moving average, and fixed level detection are sufficient.
candidate.prior_art: Inverted head-and-shoulders breakout at resistance is a widely documented pattern. Needs prior-art check for a published Long-Only Equity variant.
candidate.structural_difference: Not established. The setup matches a textbook inverted H&S breakout at a known resistance level; no unique modification or novel filter is described.
candidate.assumption_register: 1) 'Clear' breakout must be operationalized (e.g. close above 136, or 2% above, or two consecutive closes). 2) 21-day EMA must be rising (positive slope). 3) Consolidation between 136 and 21 EMA must precede the breakout. 4) Target is the prior high zone (~160s) not a fixed risk multiple. 5) The invalidation scenario (breakdown below 21 EMA → 105) is not a stop-loss but a directional invalidation.
